what_it_does: This file is the HTML entry point for the Instrument React application. It declares the document shell, viewport and theme metadata, a data-URI favicon, loads candidate web fonts, and then loads a long sequence of classic /gallery/ scripts that establish the DNA surface runtime, organism generators, card renderers, decision slide composer, face adapters, fork brain modules, and gallery hook listeners before mounting the React root via /src/main.tsx.
observations: viewport-fit=cover and user-scalable=no are set for an iOS PWA full-screen experience
theme-color #F9F7F3 matches the inline SVG favicon
five Google Fonts families are loaded with preconnect hints and a self-fallback note
a large set of classic scripts under /gallery/ is loaded in the head before the React module
comments explicitly state load-order dependencies, e.g. surface.js must come first because it hard-assigns window.DNA
organisms.js must load before unit-view.js, archetype.js after unit-view.js, and decision-render.js is required for explained decision slides
fork-brain-core.js must load before fork-v-brain.js and the gallery hook scripts

the React app is mounted on
with a type="module" script at /src/main.tsx
standouts: The file bootstraps a hybrid architecture where global classic-script runtimes (DNA and fork brain) are initialized before React mounts
Load-order comments reveal fragile global-state contracts, especially the window.DNA overwrite risk in surface.js
Gallery scripts are hosted same-origin so their /api/cognition/corpus fetch is same-origin
The viewport meta disables user scaling, prioritizing app-like behavior over accessibility
concerns: maximum-scale=1.0 and user-scalable=no reduce accessibility and may violate platform guidelines
The many classic scripts have tight ordering dependencies but no integrity attributes or async/defer controls, making the boot fragile to a single missing or reordered file
All gallery scripts are blocking in the head, which can delay first paint
There is no <noscript> or other fallback for users without JavaScript
notes: This is the bootstrap HTML for Instrument, carefully sequencing a global DNA gallery runtime and fork brain scripts ahead of the React mount.
questions: What is the S4.1 toggle and where is it implemented?
How do wildcard-gallery-binder.js and fork-gallery-brain-hooks.js differ in responsibility?
Are the /gallery/ assets versioned or cache-busted?
Is decision-render.js intentionally loaded after archetype.js even though unit-view.js gates on DNA.decisionSlide?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to serve as the app shell and load required runtimes before React mounts; the file does exactly that, with extensive ordering comments matching the script sequence.
apparent_themes: Global runtime first, React app second
intention_signals: It is trying to bootstrap a hybrid app where a React UI hosts a pre-existing DNA gallery and fork-brain classic-script runtime, ensuring all global APIs and event listeners exist before React components try to use them.
novelty: The unusual aspect is the large, carefully-ordered stack of classic global scripts loaded before a modern React module, treating window.DNA and fork brain globals as a required platform layer rather than bundling them into the React build.
contribution: This file contributes a documented, ordered bootstrap contract that initializes the DNA gallery runtime, decision renderer, face adapters, and fork brain hooks before the React application mounts, preserving the expected global API surface.
summary_for_embedding: company/surface/app/index.html is the Instrument React app's HTML shell that sets PWA metadata, loads Google Fonts, and sequences classic /gallery/ scripts for the DNA runtime, renderers, fork brain, and gallery hooks before mounting /src/main.tsx.
intention_for_embedding: It exists to establish the mobile-first document shell and ensure the DNA gallery and fork brain global runtimes are initialized in the correct order before React takes over the UI.
